message:
feat(input): implement ANSI input parsing for keyboard, mouse, and paste

This commit adds comprehensive input event parsing that converts raw
terminal byte sequences into structured events for keyboard, mouse,
paste operations, and focus changes.

src/input/mod.rs - Module Exports:
- Event enum for all input types
- InputParser for stateful sequence parsing
- KeyEvent, KeyCode, KeyModifiers
- MouseEvent, MouseButton, MouseEventKind
- FocusEvent, PasteEvent

src/input/event.rs - Event Types:
Event enum:
- Key(KeyEvent): Keyboard input
- Mouse(MouseEvent): Mouse actions
- Paste(String): Bracketed paste content
- FocusGained, FocusLost: Window focus
- Resize(u16, u16): Terminal resize

KeyEvent struct:
- code: KeyCode (the key pressed)
- modifiers: KeyModifiers (Ctrl, Alt, Shift)

KeyModifiers (bitflags):
- NONE, SHIFT, CONTROL, ALT, META
- Composable: CONTROL | SHIFT

MouseEvent struct:
- kind: MouseEventKind
- button: MouseButton
- x, y: u16 coordinates (0-based)
- modifiers: KeyModifiers

MouseEventKind enum:
- Down: Button pressed
- Up: Button released
- Drag: Motion while pressed
- Move: Motion (if tracking enabled)
- ScrollUp, ScrollDown: Wheel events

MouseButton enum:
- Left, Middle, Right
- Button4, Button5 (extra buttons)

src/input/keyboard.rs - Key Codes:
KeyCode enum:
Printable:
- Char(char): Regular characters
- Tab, Enter, Escape, Backspace

Navigation:
- Up, Down, Left, Right
- Home, End, PageUp, PageDown
- Insert, Delete

Function Keys:
- F1 through F12

Special:
- Null, CapsLock, NumLock, ScrollLock
- PrintScreen, Pause, Menu

Key Code Mapping:
- Maps CSI sequences to KeyCode values
- Handles modifier encoding in sequences
- Legacy vs modern sequence detection

src/input/parser.rs - InputParser:
InputParser struct:
- buffer: Vec<u8> for incomplete sequences
- in_paste: bool for bracketed paste state
- paste_buffer: Vec<u8> accumulating paste

parse(bytes) -> Vec<Event>:
- Consumes input bytes
- Returns zero or more parsed events
- Accumulates incomplete sequences

Sequence Types Handled:

1. ASCII (0x00-0x1F, 0x20-0x7E, 0x7F):
   - 0x1B alone: Escape key
   - 0x00-0x1A: Ctrl+A through Ctrl+Z
   - 0x20-0x7E: Printable characters
   - 0x7F: Backspace/Delete

2. UTF-8 (0x80-0xFF leading bytes):
   - Multi-byte character decoding
   - Returns Char(decoded_char)

3. CSI Sequences (\x1b[...):
   Arrow keys: \x1b[A/B/C/D
   F1-F4: \x1b[OP through \x1b[OS
   F5-F12: \x1b[15~ through \x1b[24~
   Navigation: \x1b[1~(Home), \x1b[4~(End), etc.

   Modifiers in CSI:
   - \x1b[1;2A = Shift+Up
   - \x1b[1;3A = Alt+Up
   - \x1b[1;5A = Ctrl+Up
   - Combinations: ;7 = Ctrl+Alt

4. SS3 Sequences (\x1bO...):
   - Legacy F1-F4: \x1bOP through \x1bOS
   - Some terminals use for arrows

5. Mouse (SGR and X10):
   SGR: \x1b[<btn;x;y;M (press) or m (release)
   X10: \x1b[Mbxy (legacy encoding)

   Button decoding:
   - 0: Left, 1: Middle, 2: Right
   - 64+: Scroll wheel
   - 32+: Motion flag

   Modifier bits:
   - 4: Shift, 8: Meta, 16: Ctrl

6. Bracketed Paste (\x1b[200~ ... \x1b[201~):
   - Start marker begins accumulation
   - End marker emits Paste(content)
   - Content preserved verbatim

7. Focus Events:
   - \x1b[I: FocusGained
   - \x1b[O: FocusLost

Incomplete Handling:
- Sequences split across reads are buffered
- Timeout detection for standalone Escape
- Buffer cleared when event completes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
